compliance._note_: MIRROR of locales/en.default.json compliance.* — required so 't:compliance.*' refs resolve in schema defaults. Single source of truth: MIGRATION_PLAN.md §10. Keep both files byte-identical for compliance.*.
compliance.fda_disclaimer: These statements have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.
compliance.ftc_amazon_top: As an Amazon Associate, 4WELL earns from qualifying purchases.
compliance.ftc_affiliate_footer: As an Amazon Associate, 4WELL earns from qualifying purchases. This page contains affiliate links.
compliance.not_typical_section: Photos from verified customers. Individual results vary and are not typical of every user.
compliance.video_legal: Stories from verified customers. Compensation: free product. Individual results vary and are not typical.
compliance.video_modal_legal: Verified customer. Compensation: free product. Individual results vary.
compliance.timeline_disclaimer: *Results vary by individual. Most customers report first changes within 2–4 weeks of consistent daily use.*
compliance.email_consent: By submitting, you agree to receive emails from 4WELL. Unsubscribe anytime. We never share your data.

settings.info_global_cta_url: Every Shop / Buy / Subscribe button on the page links here. Required for v1. Set to the Amazon affiliate deep-link or any single destination URL.
settings.product_panel_info: JSON-LD Product data emitted by dim-reviews (Google Rich Results). Both dim-hero and dim-reviews read product identity from these globals — single source of truth.
settings.info_product_image_url: Absolute URL; falls back to Organization logo if empty.
settings.product_description_default: DIM 370mg supplement with BioPerine, Zinc, and Niacinamide. Supports healthy estrogen metabolism for clearer skin and hormonal balance.
settings.email_panel_info: These credentials power both the inline email opt-in section AND the exit-intent popup. Leave both blank to disable email capture site-wide; the opt-in section + popup hide automatically on the storefront.
settings.info_klaviyo_company_id: Found in Klaviyo > Account > API Keys. Required for inline opt-in + exit popup. Leave blank to disable email capture.
settings.info_klaviyo_list_id: Found in Klaviyo > Lists; the URL ends with /list/XXXXXX.
settings.label_robots_index: Allow search engines to index this site
settings.info_robots_index: Leave OFF until ready for production. Turning this ON will allow Google to index this page.
settings.info_canonical_override: Leave empty to use Shopify's default. Set to the primary public URL (e.g. https://yourdomain.com/dim) if this storefront is a pre-sell landing being indexed under a different domain.
settings.info_og_image: Used for social shares (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn). 1200x630 recommended.
settings.org_panel_info: Feeds Organization JSON-LD (Knowledge Graph) emitted from the theme layout. Logo + name + social profiles help Google associate this site with the broader brand entity.

dim_hero.paragraph_cta_info: Outbound URL is set in Theme settings > Outbound CTA (single global URL).
dim_hero.info_cta_icon: Emoji or single character; blank to hide.
dim_hero.label_bottle_image: Bottle photo (image picker, overrides asset fallback)
dim_hero.info_bottle_image: Optional. Uploaded image takes priority over asset fallback below.
dim_hero.info_bottle_image_fallback: File must be present in theme/assets/ (default: hero-bottle.jpg). Used only when image picker above is empty. Clear both to fall back to the inline SVG bottle.
dim_hero.info_bottle_aria: Screen reader description of the product visual.
dim_hero.eyebrow_default: HORMONAL ACNE — THE TRUTH NOBODY TOLD YOU
dim_hero.h1_default: The Real Reason Your Chin Keeps Breaking Out — And It's Not Your Skincare
dim_hero.sub_default: Cystic chin pimples. Jawline breakouts. Monthly flare-ups. They share one underlying driver — how your body metabolizes estrogen. Here's the supplement that supports clearer skin from the inside out.

dim_announcement_countdown.info_timer_end_iso: If set (e.g. 2026-12-31T23:59:59Z), counts down to this real moment for every visitor. If blank, uses per-visitor rolling timer anchored at first visit (honest urgency).
dim_announcement_countdown.info_timer_fallback_hours: Used only when no end timestamp is set. Per-visitor anchor saved in localStorage; reload keeps the same end time.

dim_sticky_cta.info_compliance_header: Required by FTC. Default text is shared across the theme via compliance.ftc_amazon_top — change it in locale files to update all references at once.
dim_sticky_cta.info_disclosure_text: Default = compliance.ftc_amazon_top (shared verbatim). Editing here overrides only this section — generally don't.

dim_social_proof.snippet_1_quote: "My acne is finally gone after 15 years."
dim_social_proof.snippet_3_quote: "Took 6 weeks for me, but it worked."

dim_quiz.info_outcome_value: Must match one of the Q1 option values: cyclical, random, stress, constant. UTM tagged as utm_content=quiz-{outcome} when CTA is clicked.
dim_quiz.paragraph_utm_warning: Designed for qualified paid traffic. Recommended placement: gate visibility with a UTM parameter (e.g. ?utm_medium=paid) via your template logic, or display only to a paid-audience condition. Showing the quiz to organic traffic typically lowers conversion on the main funnel.
dim_quiz.h2_default: What's most likely behind your breakouts?
dim_quiz.lede_default: Four quick questions. Personalized answer at the end.
dim_quiz.result_h_default: Hormonal factors likely playing a role
dim_quiz.result_p_default: Your answers suggest hormonal factors may be contributing to your breakouts. Adult women with cyclical chin and jawline breakouts often find support in compounds that help the body metabolize estrogen — which is exactly what 4WELL DIM is formulated for.
dim_quiz.result_micro_default: Or scroll down to read why this works ↓
dim_quiz.q1_heading: When do your breakouts usually flare up?
dim_quiz.q2_heading: Where on your face do they show up most?
dim_quiz.q3_heading: Have you tried any of these?
dim_quiz.q4_heading: Which of these also sounds familiar?
dim_quiz.outcome_cyclical_h: Your skin is on a hormonal clock
dim_quiz.outcome_cyclical_p: Breakouts that arrive on schedule (the week before your period) are the textbook hormonal-acne pattern. Estrogen fluctuations drive sebum, inflammation, and chin/jaw breakouts. DIM is formulated to support how your body metabolizes estrogen — which is why cyclical breakouts are exactly the use-case it's built for.
dim_quiz.outcome_cyclical_micro: Most users notice the first shift within 2–4 weeks of consistent use.
dim_quiz.outcome_constant_h: When topicals aren't enough — look inside
dim_quiz.outcome_constant_p: If retinoids, antibiotics, and cleansers keep failing, the trigger is likely INTERNAL — not on the surface of your skin. Persistent adult breakouts almost always trace back to the hormonal pathway: DIM supports the estrogen metabolism that topical treatments can't touch.
dim_quiz.outcome_constant_micro: Full effect typically takes 60–90 days. Stay consistent.
dim_quiz.outcome_stress_h: Stress messes with your hormones — and your skin
dim_quiz.outcome_stress_p: Cortisol from chronic stress raises androgen activity, which boosts sebum and inflammation. The result: breakouts that flare during your hardest weeks. DIM supports the hormone balance that stress most reliably disrupts.
dim_quiz.outcome_stress_micro: Pair DIM with sleep + magnesium for the strongest results.
dim_quiz.outcome_random_h: Random isn't always random — there's usually a pattern underneath
dim_quiz.outcome_random_p: What looks random often masks a cycle-linked or stress-linked pattern that becomes visible only after a few months of consistent observation. DIM is a 60-day commitment that helps reveal — and improve — the pattern beneath.
dim_quiz.outcome_random_micro: Keep a quick skin journal for the first 8 weeks — patterns will emerge.

dim_advertorial.info_badge_header: Required disclosure when content is paid editorial.
dim_advertorial.info_badge_text: Default = compliance.advertorial_badge (shared). Editing here overrides only this section. Required by ad-network policies.
dim_advertorial.info_body_header: Split into 5 paragraphs (Shopify hard-limits schema string defaults to ~1024 chars per value; long-form prose must be paginated). Each block renders as its own richtext paragraph in order.
dim_advertorial.lede_default: For 12 years, I tried everything for my chin acne — every cleanser, retinoid, antibiotic, even Accutane. Nothing worked for more than 3 months. Then a dermatologist asked me one question that changed everything: "Have you tested your hormones?"
dim_advertorial.body_p1_default: I was 27 when the breakouts started. Deep, painful cysts that came back every single month, always in the same place — along my jawline and chin. By 32, I'd seen four dermatologists and tried twelve different products. Each one worked for a few weeks, then stopped. I was tired, embarrassed, and convinced something was permanently broken with my skin.
dim_advertorial.body_p2_default: The dermatologist who finally helped me didn't reach for a prescription pad. She pulled out a lab requisition form. "Adult acne in women is almost never a skin problem," she said. "It's a hormone problem wearing skin as a symptom."
dim_advertorial.body_p3_default: Research across multiple dermatology and endocrinology studies suggests a large share of adult acne in women traces back to hormonal factors rather than topical ones — specifically, an imbalance in how the body metabolizes estrogen. The result is a low-grade inflammatory pathway that surfaces, predictably, along the lower face.
dim_advertorial.body_p4_default: After weeks of research, I found one supplement that kept coming up in dermatologist forums and clinical reviews — DIM, or diindolylmethane. A compound found naturally in cruciferous vegetables, DIM is studied for its role in helping the body convert excess estrogen into a gentler metabolite. The catch? You'd need to eat pounds of raw broccoli every day to get a meaningful dose.
dim_advertorial.body_p5_default: That's when I came across 4WELL DIM — a 370mg formula with BioPerine, Zinc, and Niacinamide. What follows is what convinced me to try it, and what happened next.

dim_problem.lede_default: Hormonal acne affects roughly half of women aged 20–29 and a quarter of women aged 40–49. If any of these sound like you — your hormones are sending you a signal.
dim_problem.close_line_default: These aren't separate problems. They share one underlying factor — how efficiently your body metabolizes estrogen.
dim_problem.pain_1_text: Deep, painful cystic pimples along your jaw and chin
dim_problem.pain_2_text: Breakouts that show up like clockwork before your period
dim_problem.pain_3_text: Tried every cream, retinoid, antibiotic — nothing sticks
dim_problem.pain_4_text: Oily T-zone by 11 AM, no matter what cleanser you use
dim_problem.pain_5_text: Scarring and dark spots that take months to fade
dim_problem.pain_6_text: Heavy PMS, bloating, or mood swings alongside breakouts

dim_science.info_callout_text: Leave blank to hide the callout for this step.
dim_science.h2_default: Why your skin keeps breaking out — and what research says actually supports it
dim_science.step_1_body: Your skin reflects your hormones. When estrogen builds up faster than your body can break it down, some of that excess gets converted into 4-hydroxyestrone — a metabolite linked in research to inflammation, increased sebum production, and breakouts along the jawline and chin.
dim_science.step_2_body: DIM (diindolylmethane) is a compound naturally found in cruciferous vegetables like broccoli and cauliflower. Clinical research shows DIM helps the body convert excess estrogen into its gentler form (2-hydroxyestrone) — supporting the hormonal pathway behind adult acne.1
dim_science.step_2_callout_text: 📚 Backed by decades of peer-reviewed research on DIM and estrogen metabolism.
dim_science.step_3_body: The problem? You'd need to eat pounds of raw broccoli every day to get a meaningful dose of DIM. One 4WELL capsule delivers 370mg — paired with BioPerine®, the patented black-pepper extract studied for enhanced absorption.2

dim_timeline.info_disclaimer_header: Mandatory honest-timeline disclosure when describing expected results. Default uses shared compliance.timeline_disclaimer.
dim_timeline.info_h2: Multi-line headlines: type the literal HTML line-break tag (br in angle brackets) wherever you want a line break — for example: 'What to Expect —[br]An Honest Timeline'.
dim_timeline.info_disclaimer: Default = compliance.timeline_disclaimer (shared verbatim). Editing here overrides only this section.
dim_timeline.lede_default: The most common reason people give up on DIM too soon is not knowing what's normal in the first few weeks. Here's the real picture.
dim_timeline.step_1_bullets:
• Possible mild headache in days 1–3
  • This means hormones are beginning to shift
  • Take with food + drink plenty of water
  • Many people experience no side effects at all
dim_timeline.step_1_quote: "I had a headache on day 2 but pushed through. By day 10 I was sleeping better than I had in months."
dim_timeline.step_2_bullets:
• Less bloating & water retention
  • PMS symptoms beginning to soften
  • Sleep becoming more even
  • Skin starting to calm down
dim_timeline.step_2_quote: "Still waiting for the big changes, but I already feel less puffy. Something is happening."
dim_timeline.step_3_bullets:
• Hormonal breakouts visibly reduced
  • Energy more stable throughout the day
  • Fewer mood swings around your cycle
  • Hot flashes less frequent and less intense
dim_timeline.step_3_quote: "After about a month, I looked in the mirror and my skin was actually clear. I hadn't seen that in years."
dim_timeline.step_4_bullets:
• Clearest skin of your adult life
  • Hormonal cycle more regular
  • Feeling like yourself again
  • Energy and mood consistently stable
dim_timeline.step_4_quote: "I finally feel like myself. The difference is dramatic. I won't go a day without it."

dim_sources.info_sup_num: Must match the number used in inline footnotes from other sections (dim-science callout, dim-ingredients per-ingredient footnote_ref). Renders as superscript next to citation.
dim_sources.info_citation: Plain text + inline emphasis. Use the editor's italic for journal names (renders as em).
dim_sources.note_default: Each numerical or outcome claim on this page maps to a peer-reviewed source maintained on file with our editorial team.
dim_sources.ref_1_citation: Bradlow HL, Telang NT, Sepkovic DW, Osborne MP. 2-hydroxyestrone: the 'good' estrogen. J Endocrinol. 1996 · and subsequent DIM clinical literature on PubMed.
dim_sources.ref_2_citation: Majeed M et al. Studies on piperine and BioPerine®. Planta Medica, 1998.
dim_sources.ref_3_citation: Cervantes J, Eber AE, Perper M, Nascimento VM, Nouri K, Keri JE. The role of zinc in the treatment of acne. Dermatol Ther. 2018.
dim_sources.ref_4_citation: Walocko FM, Eber AE, Keri JE, Al-Harbi MA, Nouri K. The role of nicotinamide in acne treatment. Dermatol Ther. 2017.
dim_sources.ref_5_citation: Bhate K, Williams HC. Epidemiology of acne vulgaris. Br J Dermatol. 2013 · Khunger N, Mehrotra K. Menopausal Acne — Challenges And Solutions. Indian Dermatol Online J. 2019.

dim_faq.info_h2_emphasis: Rendered on a new line in italic. Leave empty for a single-line headline.
dim_faq.label_portrait_image: Portrait photo (image picker, overrides asset fallback)
dim_faq.info_portrait_image: Optional. Thoughtful-woman portrait, 4:5 vertical aspect. Used as the warm-human anchor next to the questions.
dim_faq.info_portrait_image_fallback: File must be present in theme/assets/ (e.g. faq-portrait.jpg). Used only when image picker above is empty. Leave both empty to hide the image entirely.
dim_faq.info_single_open: When ON: opening a Q&A automatically closes others. The native browser details element works without JS; this enforcement loads only when the section scrolls near the viewport.
dim_faq.info_hot_label: Shown next to any Q&A flagged as 'Most asked'. Empty value hides the badge globally.
dim_faq.info_answer: Richtext editor supports bold, italic, links, and lists. HTML formatting is preserved in FAQPage JSON-LD rich results.
dim_faq.info_hot: Adds a left-accent border and a small 'Most asked' badge next to the question. Recommended for the single most-objection question (e.g. side effects, safety). Use sparingly.
dim_faq.portrait_alt_default: A thoughtful woman looking into the distance
dim_faq.qa_1_question: How fast will I see results?
dim_faq.qa_1_answer: Many women notice initial changes within 2–4 weeks. Most see fuller results around weeks 6–8 of consistent daily use. For full hormonal balance, plan on 3 months. Individual results vary.
dim_faq.qa_2_answer: DIM is generally well-tolerated. Some women report mild headaches or darker urine in the first week — both are normal as your body adjusts to estrogen metabolism. If symptoms feel unusual, stop and consult your doctor.
dim_faq.qa_3_question: Can I take DIM with birth control or other medications?
dim_faq.qa_3_answer: Talk to your doctor first. DIM can affect how your body processes certain medications, including birth control. Don't combine without medical guidance.
dim_faq.qa_4_question: Is it safe for perimenopause and menopause?
dim_faq.qa_4_answer: Yes — DIM supports estrogen balance at any life stage. Many women in their 40s and 50s use it for perimenopause symptoms.
dim_faq.qa_5_answer: One capsule per day with a meal. The 30-capsule bottle is a 1-month supply.
dim_faq.qa_7_question: What if it doesn't work for me?
dim_faq.qa_7_answer: You're protected by a 30-day money-back guarantee. Email customer service for a full refund. Refund processed within 5 business days of receiving the bottle.
dim_faq.qa_8_answer: 4WELL DIM is manufactured in a GMP-certified facility in the USA. Each batch is third-party lab tested for purity, potency, and heavy metals. COA available on request.

dim_comparison.info_columns_header: Schema is fixed at 4 columns to match the source layout. Editing this requires markup change.

dim_reviews.paragraph_seo_warning: This section emits Product + AggregateRating + Review structured data (JSON-LD) consumed by Google Rich Results. Removing or disabling the section removes that schema from the page, which typically reduces eligibility for review-star rich snippets in search.
dim_reviews.header_distribution: Star distribution (percent per row, must sum to ~100)
dim_reviews.info_aggregate_count: Used in Product schema aggregateRating.reviewCount. Should match the count_label number.
dim_reviews.info_start_index: Which card is centered on first load. Default 1 = second card.
dim_reviews.label_avatar_image: Avatar photo (image picker, overrides asset + letter fallback)
dim_reviews.info_avatar_image: Optional. Real customer photo recommended. If used as JSON-LD review identity, ensure the photo matches a real verified customer to avoid FTC issues.
dim_reviews.info_avatar_fallback: File must be present in theme/assets/ (e.g. avatar-lauren.jpg). Used only when the image picker above is empty.
dim_reviews.info_avatar_letter: Single letter for circular avatar. Used only when both avatar photo and asset fallback are empty.
dim_reviews.info_stars: Integer 1-5. Used both for visual rendering and JSON-LD reviewRating.
dim_reviews.info_date_iso: Format: YYYY-MM-DD. Used as datetime= attribute and JSON-LD datePublished. Display format is locale-driven via Liquid date filter.
dim_reviews.review_1_headline: "My acne is finally gone after 15 years"
dim_reviews.review_1_body: I've tried Accutane twice. Every prescription cream. This is the only thing that's actually worked long-term. Three months in and my skin is clearer than it's been since I was 14.
dim_reviews.review_2_body: Didn't expect the bloating to fade too. PMS used to be a week of misery — now barely two days for me. And my chin breakouts? Way fewer by month two.
dim_reviews.review_3_headline: "First product that's worked for my hormonal acne"
dim_reviews.review_3_body: I'm 34 and had cystic acne since my 20s. Nothing topical ever worked for me. Started DIM in August, by November I had way fewer breakouts. Telling all my friends.
dim_reviews.review_4_headline: "Took 6 weeks for me, but it worked"
dim_reviews.review_4_body: Almost gave up around week 4 — nothing was happening. By week 6, the chin breakouts slowed way down. Wish it kicked in sooner, but sticking with it.
dim_reviews.review_5_body: Periods used to wreck my face for a week. Three months in, breakouts are minimal and my cycle is way more predictable.

footer_minimal.info_compliance_header: Required by FDA & FTC. Defaults pull from compliance.fda_disclaimer + compliance.ftc_affiliate_footer + compliance.editorial_sponsor (shared verbatim). Editing here overrides only this footer.
footer_minimal.info_copyright_text: Use [year] as a placeholder for the current year — auto-substituted at render time.
footer_minimal.info_nav_url: Leave blank in v1 (renders as '#'). For production: link to merchant-owned pages — privacy policy, terms of service, contact, refund policy. Internal anchors such as #refs also work.
